VerbalDescription: draw the box above the horizontal line and i follow the Task demonstration
There will be a solid or dotted line across the entire screen in an opposing color, simply drop all shapes below that line, keep colors and grid size the same
Use the same grid size as in the input. Copy all objects above horizontal line and copy horizontal line (keep all colors the same).

Use the same grid size as in the input. There will be multiple objects separated by a horizontal object that will be of a different color to the rest of the objects. Please modify every object under this horizontal object to the color black.
Copy the input from the demontration screen. Remove all shapes from beneath the horizontal line shape.

Use exact grid dimensions as demonstration/input. Determine which objects are above the line (the single row of grid squares, either all connected, or alternating) cutting across the entire grid. Only keep those objects which are above the line. Recreate the line exactly as it appears in the demonstration/input in your solution. Recreate exact copies of only those objects above the line in your solution, keeping their dimensions, color, and shape. Place them in the exact same location as the input in your solution.

The rule is to remove all of the shapes that are underneath the row that goes across (in this case, the yellow row). To do this, copy the original grid from input and then select 'flood fill'. Then select the black color and click on any purple square in all 3 of the large purple shapes underneath the yellow row. This should turn every square on the bottom of the yellow row black.

so there should be 2 sets of blocks seperated by a line. you just copy from grid and then delete whatever is below the line.

The grid size should match the input. There is a dividing line, and everything above the dividing line should be preserved, and everything below the dividing line should be deleted/filled in with black squares.

Use the 'copy from input' tool to copy the input and grid dimensions. There should be a horizontal line drawn, which may be a complete line or a broken line. You should only keep the shapes drawn above this line. If there are shapes below this line, change the squares to black to remove them.

Using the same sized grid, remove anything under the line. If the underside is already empty, leave as is.

Choose 'Copy from input' button. Choose 'Select' and draw a box that contains all of the cells that are below the horizontal line. Click the black color to erase all cells below the line.

Everything below the 'line' needs to be removed

This is a fill rule, it fills the area between the line and the edges.  So copy the input, select the same colour as the line, and then pick a black square that is below the line and let it fill the rest.

Ok so here the rule is that the opposing colour in the original image takes over the colour of any blocks beneath it. So in this case, the red blocks on top of the blue blocks took over the blue blocks. 

To solve this puzzle what I did was copy the original design and then replace the blue blocks beneath the red blocks with red blocks. Before doing this I also resized the grid to 9X7 to replicate the dimensions of the original. I then used the edit function and recoloured the originally blue blocks into red. This left 3 red downward strips and 1 blue downward strip.

The rule is to extend the red shapes downward to completely fill in all squares directly underneath them. To do this, copy the original grid from input, choose 'flood fill' and then the red color and select one of the blue squares from the 2 left columns. This should fill both columns with red. Then choose 'edit' and the red color and select the 5 blue squares directly underneath the red square on the right side. That should make that entire column red, leaving you with only 1 blue column on the right side.

Copy out the grid from the input. For any colours that are not red or black and are underneath the red coloured squares, colour them in red. If the squares are above the red squares, then leave them as normal.
